name: Steamed Fresh Crab in Curry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the sauce: Heat peanut oil in a medium-hot saucepan. Saute minced garlic until fragrant. Add curry powder and stir over medium heat for about 1 minute—avoid burning the powder. Add stock, salt, sugar, and ginger; bring to boil. Cook for 1 minute. Add diced bell pepper (about 1/4-inch squares) and stir in enough cornstarch paste to make a light sauce. Remove from heat when thickened and set aside.
2. Clean & Steam Crab: Pry off the shell plate from the crab in one piece. Separate legs with a cleaver; wash all parts, removing debris. Cut soft inner sections of legs into manageable pieces. Carefully crack claws, keeping them intact. Reassemble the crab on an oval platter. Sprinkle meat with dry sherry before placing it back on the platter. Lay ginger slices on top.
3. Steam the crab: Bring water to a rolling boil in a steamer. Steam the crab for about 15-20 minutes, depending on size. Meanwhile, reheat the curry sauce (or keep hot in a double boiler).
4. Serve: Remove the steamed crab from the steamer and drain excess water. Pour the prepared curry sauce over the crab and sprinkle with shredded green onions. Return the platter to serve.
